verb_relations.sentence: However, in many ways, technology has profoundly changed education.

verb_relations.subject: technology
verb_relations.sentence: For one, technology has greatly expanded access to education.

verb_relations.subject: few
verb_relations.sentence: In medieval times, books were rare and only an elite few had access to educational opportunities.

verb_relations.subject: Individuals
verb_relations.sentence: Individuals had to travel to centers of learning to get an education.

verb_relations.subject: Access
verb_relations.sentence: Access to learning opportunities today is unprecedented in scope thanks to technology.

verb_relations.subject: technology
verb_relations.sentence: Today, technology enables forms of communication and collaboration undreamt of in the past.

verb_relations.subject: Students
verb_relations.sentence: Students in a classroom in the rural U.S., for example, can learn about the Arctic by following the expedition of a team of scientists in the region, read scientists’ blog posting, view photos, e-mail questions to the scientists, and even talk live with the scientists via a videoconference.

verb_relations.subject: who
verb_relations.sentence: Students can share what they are learning with students in other classrooms in other states who are tracking the same expedition.

verb_relations.subject: Students
verb_relations.sentence: Students can collaborate on group projects using technology-based tools such as wikis and Google docs.

verb_relations.subject: technology
verb_relations.sentence: The walls of the classrooms are no longer a barrier as technology enables new ways of learning, communicating, and working collaboratively.

verb_relations.subject: Technology
verb_relations.sentence: Technology has also begun to change the roles of teachers and learners.

verb_relations.subject: learners
verb_relations.sentence: In the traditional classroom, such as what we see depicted in de Voltolina’s illustration, the teacher is the primary source of information, and the learners passively receive it.

verb_relations.subject: students
verb_relations.sentence: However, because of the access to information and educational opportunity that technology has enabled, in many classrooms today we see the teacher’s role shifting to the “guide on the side” as students take more responsibility for their own learning using technology to gather relevant information.

verb_relations.subject: Schools
verb_relations.sentence: Schools and universities across the country are beginning to redesign learning spaces to enable this new model of education, foster more interaction and small group work, and use technology as an enabler.

verb_relations.subject: people
verb_relations.sentence: Technology is a powerful tool that can support and transform education in many ways, from making it easier for teachers to create instructional materials to enabling new ways for people to learn and work together.

verb_relations.subject: education
verb_relations.sentence: It will be up to instructional designers and educational technologies to make the most of the opportunities provided by technology to change education so that effective and efficient education is available to everyone everywhere.

verb_relations.subject: You
verb_relations.sentence: You can help shape the influence of technology in education with an Online Master of Science in Education in Learning Design and Technology from Purdue University Online.

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: This accredited program offers studies in exciting new technologies that are shaping education and offers students the opportunity to take part in the future of innovation.

verb_relations.subject: individuals
verb_relations.sentence: Learn more about the online MSEd in Learning Design and Technology at Purdue University today and help redefine the way in which individuals learn.
